title: Kiss-of-Death
setup: Your knight on **f6** stops the king from escaping to **g8**, and your king on **g6** defends the queen's landing square.
hint: Drop the queen right next to the king — she's defended by your king and the knight covers {{g8}}.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is the famous **kiss-of-death** mate: the queen lands on the square *adjacent* to the enemy king, defended by your own king. The knight handles the corner escape.

Play {{Qh7#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- {{g8}} — controlled by the knight on **f6** (knight jumps to g8)
- {{g7}} — controlled by your king on **g6** *and* by the queen herself
- The queen on h7 attacks h8 directly (check)
- {{Kxh7}} is illegal because your king on **g6** defends h7
- Nothing can block: h7 is adjacent to h8

> **Takeaway:** Memorize the *kiss-of-death* pattern — queen + king two squares apart, with a third piece (here, the knight) covering the only flight square. It appears in countless games, especially after a pawn has been pushed forward to f6 or g6.
